name: 陈淑珊
current_positions: Member of the Parliament of Singapore (NMP 2012-2014)

work_experience.position: Institutional equity and derivative sales (London, Tokyo, Hong Kong)
Regional Head for Brunei, Malaysia and Singapore
Head of Private Wealth Management, Southeast Asia
Group Head of Consumer Banking and Wealth Management
Deputy CEO and Group Head of Institutional Banking

person_relationships.description: Succeeded Piyush Gupta as CEO of DBS Group in March 2025 after he led the bank for 14 years
Married to Christopher Wilson; two children, Talisa and Kai

political_stances.stance_content: Advocated for AI and crypto integration in banking, preparing DBS for digital transformation while managing service disruptions

major_achievements.achievement: Named world's Best Leader in Private Banking at PWM/The Banker Global Private Banking Awards in Geneva, the first Singaporean to receive this honor
Featured on Forbes Asia's Power Businesswomen List
Became first female CEO of DBS Bank, Southeast Asia's largest bank with $637 billion in assets
Ranked 6th globally in Fortune's Most Powerful Women in Business list, the only Singaporean in the top 10; also ranked 28th on Fortune's inaugural '100 Most Powerful People in Business' list

biography_summary: Tan Su Shan (born 1968) is a Singaporean banker who has served as CEO and Director of DBS Group since March 2025, becoming the first female CEO of Southeast Asia's largest bank. She graduated from Lincoln College, Oxford with an MA in Politics, Philosophy and Economics in 1989. Her 35+ year banking career spans ING Baring Securities, Morgan Stanley, Citigroup, and DBS. She joined DBS in 2010, built its wealth management business, and later led both Consumer Banking and Institutional Banking divisions. She was a Nominated Member of Parliament (2012-2014). In 2025, she was ranked 6th globally in Fortune's Most Powerful Women in Business list. She is married to Christopher Wilson with two children.

collection_meta.notes: Enriched with full career timeline across ING Barings, Morgan Stanley, Citigroup and DBS. Added family members, major awards including Fortune ranking.
